source.title: Silver Eagle Refinery Explosion Investigation: Metallurgical Analysis

actions_taken: Site inspection and documentation conducted January 7-8, 2010
Pipe components sectioned and removed for metallurgical analysis March 29-30, 2010
Ultrasonic thickness testing performed on fractured pipe segments
Quantitative chemical analysis conducted on pipe specimens
Metallographic examination of fracture surfaces performed
Security video analysis of rupture and explosion sequence
event.summary: On November 4, 2009, a sudden and complete rupture occurred in the 10-inch diameter discharge line at the exit of the South Reactor (R30101) in the Mobil Distillate Dewaxing Unit (MDDW) during hot hydrogen catalyst regeneration. The rupture released approximately 175.3 kg of hydrogen-rich gas mixture at 642 psig and 785°F. The released gas ignited approximately 0.66 seconds after rupture, causing an explosion that damaged residential homes in the adjacent community. Metallurgical analysis revealed wall thinning from the inside diameter through the weld metal and base metal of the 10-inch straight segment, consistent with sulfidation corrosion. The piping had been in service since 1993 with no documented wall thickness monitoring between installation and the incident.
recommendations: Implement corrosion monitoring program for low-alloy carbon steel piping in sulfidation-prone service
Establish baseline wall thickness measurements and periodic ultrasonic inspection intervals
Consider material upgrades to molybdenum-containing alloys or stainless steel in high-temperature sulfur-containing service
Review process stream chemistry and operating conditions for sulfidation corrosion risk
Document original pipe specifications and maintain inspection records

hazards.name: High-temperature, high-pressure hydrogen-rich gas in process piping

threats.name: Sulfidation corrosion of low-alloy carbon steel piping
Wall thinning from internal corrosion attack
Pipe rupture due to loss of structural integrity

consequences.name: Loss of containment - uncontrolled gas release

controls.name: Pipe material selection - low-alloy carbon steel (ASTM A213 Grade T12)

supporting_text: The compositions of the specimens from the 10-inch straight segment were consistent with the specifications current in 1966 for ASTM A213, Grade T12 alloy steel
Low-alloy carbon steel with low levels of silicon, chromium, and molybdenum are particularly vulnerable

controls.name: Ultrasonic thickness inspection program
controls.barrier_role: Detection of wall thinning before rupture

supporting_text: It has not been determined if the 10-inch elbow and straight segment were new at the time of the 1993 installation
to date no documentation has been produced indicating that the wall thickness of the straight segment was ever measured from build date to the time of the incident
It appears that corrosion monitoring of the straight segment was not conducted between 1993 and the time of the rupture

controls.name: Process stream chemistry control - sulfur content management
controls.barrier_role: Reduce corrosive species in process stream

supporting_text: A 'Diesel Hydrotreater Startup Report' issued by Albemarle Catalysts following a turnaround in April 2006 indicated that the 'straight run diesel from the crude unit had a sulfur content of 1.0 wt%'
hydrogen sulfide (H2S) concentration in the recycle gas 'exceeded 0.5 vol%' during the sulfiding
Pipe supports underneath the pipe are illustrated in Figure 17, with the longest unsupported run of pipe measuring just less than twelve feet
Inadequate pipe support is not considered a contributing factor to this accident

controls.name: Pressure relief and depressurization systems
controls.barrier_role: Limit pressure and gas release rate

supporting_text: Pressure in R30101 was increased from normal operating levels just below 600 psig to about 640 psi during the catalyst regeneration process

controls.name: Ignition source control and hot work procedures
controls.barrier_role: Prevent ignition of released flammable gas

supporting_text: the hot gas mixture ignited shortly after rupture
Analysis of the security video revealed that the time between pipe rupture and the initial explosion was approximately 0.66 seconds

controls.name: Blast containment and separation distance
controls.barrier_role: Limit blast damage to adjacent areas

supporting_text: A shock wave from the resulting explosion expanded through the adjacent neighborhood, causing varied degrees of blast damage to residential homes
A view from the hydro pad includes the surrounding residential community

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
